name: why-im-no-longer-talking-to-white-people-about-race
description: Reni Eddo-Lodge's groundbreaking examination of structural racism in Britain — from the erasure of Black British history to white privilege, intersectional feminism, and the urgent need to confront race honestly.
useCase: Understanding structural racism in Britain, the history of Black British struggle, white privilege as a systemic concept, intersectional feminism, and the class-race debate.
knownRisks: Written from a British perspective — some examples may not translate to other countries
The title and thesis have been criticized as divisive
Some historical claims have been disputed by other scholars
Explicitly challenges comfortable liberal assumptions about race
